@graph.name: BMW models fitted with the BMW B57/B58

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW B57 or B58 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned B57 (diesel) costs £5,000–£8,000, while the B58 (petrol) is £3,800–£6,000 supply only [BMW-QUOTE], varying by sub‑variant and vehicle. Fitting adds £650–£1,300. Use our calculator for your specific model.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW B57/B58 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B57 and B58 earn 4★ out of 5 [BMW-VERIFIED], based on 1,000+ combined UK enquiries. They are widely considered BMW’s most reliable modern sixes, with fewer catastrophic failures than the N47/N57.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common BMW B57/B58 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Coolant system issues are the most common — water pump leaks, expansion tank cracks, and plastic fittings failing. These typically occur from 60,000–120,000 miles and are relatively inexpensive to fix.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW models use the B57 or B58 engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B57 diesel powers 20d, 30d, 40d models across the 1–8 Series and X‑range; the B58 petrol powers 30i, 40i and M Performance models like the M340i, M440i, M240i, X3 M40i and Z4 M40i.

mainEntity.name: How long does the BMW B57/B58 engine last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With proper servicing (10,000‑mile oil changes using BMW Longlife‑17 spec), both can comfortably exceed 180,000–220,000 miles. The B58’s open‑deck block and B57’s steel‑piston design are built for longevity.

hero.h1: BMW B57/B58 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The BMW B57 diesel and B58 petrol are the modern modular straight‑sixes — robust and efficient, but each has its own known issues. Backed by 1,000+ combined real UK enquiries in 2025 [BMW‑VERIFIED].

verdictRating.confidence: 90% confidence — based on 1,000+ combined UK enquiries, specialist reports, and manufacturer TSBs.

rows.score: 16 (4★ × 4 = 16 — both are significantly more reliable than their N‑series predecessors)
16 (Most issues are non‑catastrophic (coolant leaks, sensors) and well‑understood)
20 (Modern modular engines; parts abundant through BMW and aftermarket)
10 (Recon cost as % of lowest host‑value (£15k) ~43% (30‑44% band) – conservative watch)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The B57 and B58 are BMW’s most refined modern sixes — robust and efficient — but coolant system issues are the main watchpoint; budget for preventative maintenance.
verdictRating.bestFor: High‑mileage drivers wanting strong economy (B57) or enthusiasts seeking smooth power (B58).
verdictRating.avoidIf: You’re unprepared for occasional cooling‑system repairs (water pump, expansion tank) from 60,000 miles.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Exclusive to BMW — no cross‑brand fitment.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting a B57 or B58 typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [BMW‑QUOTE].
